channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Justice Department 'anti-weaponization' fund paused
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to pause the $1.8 billion fund's creation. The DOJ says the fund is for people who believe they have been persecuted politically.
item.content:encoded: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to pause the $1.8 billion fund's creation. The DOJ says the fund is for people who believe they have been persecuted politically.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach

item.title: Despite continued fighting, U.S. says deal to end Iran war is progressing
item.description: The ceasefire with Iran is under strain. In Lebanon and Gaza, the truce deals exist only on paper.
item.content:encoded: The ceasefire with Iran is under strain. In Lebanon and Gaza, the truce deals exist only on paper.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Expert discusses the possibility for a deal to end the war with Iran
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Ali Vaez, director of the Iran project at the International Crisis Group, about the Trump administration's efforts to end the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Ali Vaez, director of the Iran project at the International Crisis Group, about the Trump administration's efforts to end the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: A look at the race to become the next mayor of Los Angeles
item.description: In the upcoming primary for mayor of Los Angeles, incumbent Karen Bass is defending from two main challengers: a conservative reality TV star and a progressive city council member.
item.content:encoded: In the upcoming primary for mayor of Los Angeles, incumbent Karen Bass is defending from two main challengers: a conservative reality TV star and a progressive city council member.
item.dc:creator: Frank Stoltze

item.title: Bondi doubles down on her handling of Epstein files in testimony to Congress
item.description: In a closed-door interview, Bondi said there were redaction errors, but otherwise defended the Justice Department's release of files in the Epstein case. She was ousted as attorney general in April.
item.content:encoded:
In a closed-door interview, Bondi said there were redaction errors, but otherwise defended the Justice Department's release of files in the Epstein case. She was ousted as attorney general in April.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The U.S. says it's moving closer to a deal to end the Iran war despite continued fighting, DOJ opens investigation into Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll, Bari Weiss wants to reinvent CBS' "60 Minutes."
item.content:encoded: The U.S. says it's moving closer to a deal to end the Iran war despite continued fighting, DOJ opens investigation into Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll, Bari Weiss wants to reinvent CBS' "60 Minutes."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Béla Fleck and Renée Fleming join forces to celebrate Appalachian music on new album
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with banjo virtuoso Béla Fleck and soprano Renée Fleming about their collaborative project and album, "The Fiddle and The Drum," which celebrates Appalachian folk music.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with banjo virtuoso Béla Fleck and soprano Renée Fleming about their collaborative project and album, "The Fiddle and The Drum," which celebrates Appalachian folk music.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Texas State Democratic Rep. James Talarico on race to flip Senate seat
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Texas State Democratic Rep. James Talarico in the aftermath of the primary, which solidified Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton as his GOP competitor in November.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Texas State Democratic Rep. James Talarico in the aftermath of the primary, which solidified Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton as his GOP competitor in November.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: The Corporate Transparency Act once had bipartisan support. What happened?
item.description: The Corporate Transparency Act was supposed to shed light on shell companies. It had bipartisan support, until it didn't. Why does the Trump administration want to shelve it?
item.content:encoded: The Corporate Transparency Act was supposed to shed light on shell companies. It had bipartisan support, until it didn't. Why does the Trump administration want to shelve it?
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: A look at the race to become the next mayor of Los Angeles
item.description: In the upcoming primary for Mayor of Los Angeles, incumbent Karen Bass is defending from two wildly different frontrunning challengers. Conservative reality TV star Spencer Pratt, and progressive city council member Nithya Raman.
item.content:encoded: In the upcoming primary for Mayor of Los Angeles, incumbent Karen Bass is defending from two wildly different frontrunning challengers. Conservative reality TV star Spencer Pratt, and progressive city council member Nithya Raman.
item.dc:creator: Frank Stoltze
